testcase.name: StynxPrivacyModule integration > loads PII rules, exports live and archive rows, erases data, and reports retention
privacy runtime helpers > generates ROPA markdown with defaults and data-flow metadata
privacy runtime helpers > wraps privacy errors with stable codes and optional context
privacy runtime helpers > loads database PII rules, auth user defaults, overrides, and validates subject metadata
privacy runtime helpers > throws when loaded PII rules still lack subject metadata
privacy runtime helpers > loads override-only PII rules and falls back to the process working directory
privacy runtime helpers > renders data-flow metadata when controllers or processors are provided alone
privacy runtime helpers > emits the exact ROPA header block (kills StringLiteral mutants on lines 15-17)
privacy runtime helpers > emits ROPA rows with the exact pipe-delimited template (kills StringLiteral mutants on the row builder)
privacy runtime helpers > falls back to "both" retention target string when target is undefined
privacy runtime helpers > falls back to "unspecified" when category and subjectColumn are absent
privacy runtime helpers > falls back to "none" retention literal when rule.retention is undefined
privacy runtime helpers > emits "## Data Flow" section header with exact text when controllers OR processors present
privacy runtime helpers > omits Data Flow section entirely when both controllers and processors are empty arrays
privacy runtime helpers > omits Data Flow section when controllers/processors are undefined (no metadata key)
privacy runtime helpers > emits Data Flow with controllers only when controllers.length > 0 and processors.length === 0
privacy runtime helpers > emits Data Flow with processors only when processors.length > 0 and controllers.length === 0
privacy runtime helpers > terminates the markdown with a trailing newline (kills StringLiteral mutation on the final empty line)
privacy runtime helpers > rejects DB rules whose tableSchema starts with a digit (kills Regex ^ anchor)
privacy runtime helpers > rejects DB rules whose tableName contains a dash (kills Regex $ anchor + .regex MethodExpression)
privacy runtime helpers > rejects DB rules whose columnName contains whitespace
privacy runtime helpers > rejects DB rules with an unknown strategy (kills enum MethodExpression mutants)
privacy runtime helpers > applies the auth/users subjectColumn="id" default (kills ObjectLiteral + LogicalOperator on line 64)
privacy runtime helpers > does NOT apply the auth/users default when schema differs (kills LogicalOperator || mutation on line 64)
privacy runtime helpers > does NOT apply the auth/users default when table differs (catches the second half of the && condition)
privacy runtime helpers > sorts loaded rules by schema.table.column lexicographically (kills BooleanLiteral mutants on the sort comparator)
privacy runtime helpers > includes the column name in the PrivacyConfigurationError context (kills StringLiteral mutants on lines 103-105)
privacy runtime helpers > delegates privacy object store operations with configured defaults and overrides
PrivacyController API contract > delegates export, erasure, and retention requests to the privacy service
PrivacyController API contract > keeps privacy mutations explicitly non-idempotent
PrivacyService branch coverage > rejects export requests without a selector
PrivacyService branch coverage > exports an empty bundle when rules match no rows
PrivacyService branch coverage > exports live and archive rows using subject and tenant filters
PrivacyService branch coverage > exports unfiltered table snapshots and uses the default export TTL
PrivacyService branch coverage > skips export rules that do not describe a subject column
PrivacyService branch coverage > fails erasure when a rule lacks subject metadata
PrivacyService branch coverage > applies every erasure strategy to live and archive targets and disables Cognito user
PrivacyService branch coverage > treats missing row counts as zero during erasure
PrivacyService branch coverage > executes retention delete branches across live and archive targets
PrivacyService branch coverage > keeps dry-run retention from deleting and rejects unsafe identifiers
PrivacyService branch coverage > treats empty retention count results as zero and defaults target to both
PrivacyService branch coverage > renders ROPA markdown from loaded rules
PrivacyService — exportData CSV escaping (kills csvEscape mutants at src/privacy.service.ts:39-48) > passes plain strings through verbatim (no quoting)
PrivacyService — exportData CSV escaping (kills csvEscape mutants at src/privacy.service.ts:39-48) > escapes strings containing commas by wrapping in double quotes
PrivacyService — exportData CSV escaping (kills csvEscape mutants at src/privacy.service.ts:39-48) > escapes strings containing newlines by wrapping in double quotes
PrivacyService — exportData CSV escaping (kills csvEscape mutants at src/privacy.service.ts:39-48) > doubles embedded double-quote characters within a quoted field
PrivacyService — exportData CSV escaping (kills csvEscape mutants at src/privacy.service.ts:39-48) > emits the literal empty string for null and undefined cell values
PrivacyService — exportData CSV escaping (kills csvEscape mutants at src/privacy.service.ts:39-48) > JSON-encodes non-string non-null values and wraps them when they contain commas
PrivacyService — manifest null coalescing (kills LogicalOperator survivors at src/privacy.service.ts:109-110) > coalesces absent subjectUserId to null in manifest.json (not undefined)
PrivacyService — manifest null coalescing (kills LogicalOperator survivors at src/privacy.service.ts:109-110) > coalesces absent tenantId to null in manifest.json (not undefined)
PrivacyService — expiresAt arithmetic (kills ArithmeticOperator survivors at src/privacy.service.ts:134) > sets expiresAt to Date.now() + (exportTtlSeconds * 1000)
PrivacyService — expiresAt arithmetic (kills ArithmeticOperator survivors at src/privacy.service.ts:134) > uses the 7-day default when exportTtlSeconds is omitted
PrivacyService — empty-side guard on exportData (kills ConditionalExpression at src/privacy.service.ts:91) > includes a table when only liveRows are present (archive empty)
PrivacyService — empty-side guard on exportData (kills ConditionalExpression at src/privacy.service.ts:91) > includes a table when only archiveRows are present (live empty)
PrivacyService — empty-side guard on exportData (kills ConditionalExpression at src/privacy.service.ts:91) > skips a table only when BOTH sides are empty
